description: The role a single set plays (§5.5). Purely structural — position in a scheme, not terminal state. Spec canon: warmup, working, drop, backoff (with a named tail: cluster, myo, primer). Extended with rest_pause and top_set so the intra-set-rest family and top-set/back-off programming encode consistently. Terminal state (e.g. taken to failure) is a separate, orthogonal axis — see `terminated-by.json` (WorkUnit.performance.terminatedBy, §5.5) — and MAY be combined with any role here (e.g. a drop-set component taken to failure is `setRole: "drop"` + `terminatedBy: "to_failure"`).

tokens.label: Warm-up Set
tokens.description: A submaximal preparatory set, typically excluded from working volume.

tokens.label: Working Set
tokens.description: A counted set at the prescribed effort/load.

tokens.label: Top Set
tokens.description: The heaviest set of an exercise (e.g. the AMRAP top set in 5/3/1).

tokens.label: Back-off Set
tokens.description: A lighter set following a heavier top set.

tokens.label: Drop Set Component
tokens.description: A post-first set at reduced load within a drop_set Block.

tokens.label: Rest-Pause Component
tokens.description: A mini-set within a rest-pause Block, separated by a short rest.

tokens.label: Cluster Component
tokens.description: A mini-set within a cluster set, separated by a short intra-set rest.

tokens.label: Myo-rep Component
tokens.description: An activation or mini-set within a myo-reps scheme.

tokens.label: Primer Set
tokens.description: A light potentiation/priming set before working sets.
